questions.assertion: A company records its transactions in the order in which they occur.
questions.reason: This is in accordance with the accrual basis of accounting.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: This is because recording transactions in the order they occur helps in matching the revenues and expenses in the same period.

questions.assertion: A cash book is a part of the ledger.
questions.reason: It is a separate book maintained to record all cash transactions.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Although a cash book is used to record cash transactions, it is not a part of the ledger.

questions.assertion: A cash book is a part of the journal.
questions.reason: It is a separate book maintained to record all cash transactions.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Although a cash book is used to record cash transactions, it is not a part of the journal.

questions.assertion: A company can record its transactions either by cash or by accrual basis of accounting.
questions.reason: The company's management decides the basis of accounting.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The company's management decides the basis of accounting based on its requirements and business needs.

questions.assertion: A journal is a book in which all business transactions are recorded first.
questions.reason: This is the first step in the accounting process.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Recording transactions in the journal is the first step in the accounting process, also known as the journal entry.

questions.assertion: The accounting equation is Assets = Liabilities + Capital.
questions.reason: This equation is used to check the accuracy of the ledger accounts.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: The accounting equation is used to check the accuracy of the ledger accounts and to ensure the consistency of the accounting records.

questions.assertion: A cash book is a journal in itself.
questions.reason: It is a separate book maintained to record all cash transactions.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Although a cash book records cash transactions, it is not a journal.

questions.assertion: A journal entry is made in the ledger, not in the journal.
questions.reason: This is not the correct process of recording transactions.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: A journal entry is recorded in the journal, and then the ledger accounts are updated based on the journal entry.

questions.assertion: A cash book is a part of the general ledger.
questions.reason: It is a separate book maintained to record all cash transactions.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Although a cash book records cash transactions, it is not a part of the general ledger.

questions.assertion: A journal is a book in which all business transactions are recorded finally.
questions.reason: This is the last step in the accounting process.
opts: (A) Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A
(B) Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A
(C) A is true but R is false
(D) A is false but R is true
questions.explanation: Recording transactions in the journal is the first step in the accounting process, not the last.
